description: Port 3000 is occupied by a standard dummy process.

cleanup_command: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y npm || true
description: NPM is uninstalled from the system, causing 'npm install' to fail.
A package is installed without its required peer dependency (React), causing build/start scripts to fail.
The package.json contains a syntax error (trailing comma), causing npm commands to fail immediately when parsing.

category: Network - Zombie Process with Ignored Signals (Medium)
break_command:
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/zombie.js
process.on('SIGTERM', () => { console.log('Ignoring termination...'); });nsetInterval(() => {}, 1000);
EOF
sudo nohup node /tmp/zombie.js > /dev/null 2>&1 &
description: A Node process occupies resources but ignores standard SIGTERM kill signals, requiring a SIGKILL (-9).

break_command: mkdir -p app/node_modules && sudo chown root:root app/node_modules
cleanup_command: sudo chown -R ubuntu:ubuntu app || true && sudo rm -rf app
description: The node_modules directory is owned by root, causing 'npm install' to fail with EACCES permission denied errors.

cleanup_command: sudo iptables -D INPUT -p tcp --dport 3000 -j REJECT || true
description: The server's internal firewall (iptables) is configured to actively reject traffic on port 3000.

break_command: mkdir -p app && echo '{"scripts": {"dev": "next dev"}}' > app/package.json && sudo chattr +i app/package.json || true
cleanup_command: sudo chattr -i app/package.json || true && sudo rm -rf app
description: The package.json file is made immutable. NPM cannot update it, and Git cannot overwrite it.

break_command: sudo mv /usr/bin/npm /usr/bin/npm-backup || true && echo 'echo "npm is broken" && exit 1' | sudo tee /usr/bin/npm > /dev/null && sudo chmod +x /usr/bin/npm
description: NPM exists in the PATH, but the binary is replaced by a malicious/broken bash script.
The maximum number of open files is restricted to 10, causing NPM and Node to crash instantly during module resolution.
